_about: THE single source of truth for the display palette. One named palette per theme; each lists every colour token. tokens.css and the PWA manifest are GENERATED from this file by `python -m catalogue.webui.theme.gen` — never hand-edit those; change a colour here and regenerate. A drift/losslessness test (tests/test_palette_master.py) asserts the committed outputs equal a fresh regen. To add a theme (e.g. 'sepia'), add a palette block here, regenerate, and select it at runtime with <html data-theme="sepia">. NOTE: covers.py keeps its own per-title cover-art palette deliberately separate; reader-chrome tokens (--bar/--reader-bg) stay reader-local and are not here.
_reading_about: In-book READER themes (Apple Books style: White/Sepia/Gray/Night), kept separate from the app `palettes` above (the reader chrome is reader-local by design). Generated into static/reader/reader-themes.css and CatalogueDesign/Generated/ReadingPalette.swift; selected at runtime with <body data-reader-theme="…"> (web/PWA) or ReadingTheme (iOS). `scheme` drives color-scheme + PDF night-invert.
